factors.summary: The company is facing material litigation, including shareholder class action lawsuits and derivative lawsuits.

evidence.text: In August 2018, a putative shareholder class action lawsuit was filed... The actions were consolidated... Nielsen intends to defend these lawsuits vigorously.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is facing significant litigation, including shareholder class actions, which poses a major risk.

factors.summary: The company is not facing significant environmental compliance costs or operational constraints.

evidence.text: There were no unregistered sales of our common stock during the three months ended September 30, 2020.
evidence.subsection: Income Taxes (Topic 740): Simplifying the Accounting for Income taxes

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No significant environmental compliance issues were reported, indicating stability but no positive developments.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is stable; however, new orders are lagging revenue due to the impact of COVID-19.

evidence.text: For further discussion regarding the potential impacts of the Transaction on the Company, see "Part II—Item 1A—Risk Factors."
evidence.subsection: Critical Accounting Policies / Goodwill and Indefinite-Lived Intangible Assets

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: New orders are lagging due to COVID-19, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable; there is no mention of price changes or customer responses to pricing actions.

evidence.text: The following discussion and analysis should also be read together with the accompanying Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements and related notes thereto.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is stable with no changes mentioned, showing no clear trend.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards emerging markets, which is beneficial for growth.

evidence.text: We continue to look for growth opportunities through global expansion, specifically within emerging markets, as well as through the cross-platform expansion of our analytical services and measurement services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting revenue mix towards emerging markets suggests beneficial growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are declining due to the impact of COVID-19 on various operations.

evidence.text: For Nielsen Global Media, the health of our consumer panels which are a very important source of measurement data have and will continue to be impacted because our field workers are constrained from their activities relating to recruiting, metering and monitoring the panelist homes. For Nielsen Global Connect, this was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on retail measurement services in markets that are heavy in traditional trade as well as pressures on custom insights and physical work conducted in stores innovation. These pressures are expected to continue, primarily due to non-contracted revenues in both Nielsen Global Media and Nielsen Global Connect.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes due to COVID-19 impact indicates a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with no significant mention of wage inflation or benefit expense issues.

evidence.text: We have taken measures to protect the health and safety of our employees, their families and our clients, with a large majority of our worldwide workforce working from home.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable with no significant issues noted, indicating stability.

factors.summary: Supply chain is disrupted due to the impact of COVID-19, with field research and in-person client engagements halted in many markets.

evidence.text: We have halted in-store field research and in-person client engagements in many markets and are adapting processes and developing innovative solutions to ensure continuity of critical business processes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: COVID-19 has disrupted the supply chain, causing field research halts and client engagement issues, indicating significant operational challenges.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is unclear from the text, but restructuring may signal reduced capacity.

evidence.text: We expect the Restructuring Plan to be substantially completed in 2020 and for restructuring actions and other permanent cost-savings initiatives to drive approximately $250 million in pre-tax annual run-rate savings.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring signals potential reduction in capacity utilization.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: The following discussion and analysis should also be read together with the accompanying Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements and related notes thereto.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are not explicitly mentioned in the text.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety incidents are mentioned in the text.

evidence.text: The following discussion and analysis should also be read together with the accompanying Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements and related notes thereto.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No incidents mentioned, but no positive information either.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious, with a focus on cost management and restructuring despite long-term strategic goals.

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cautious tone but maintaining strategic initiatives.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on geographic expansion, service offering expansion, and cost efficiency.

evidence.text: We are focused on geographic market and service offering expansion to drive revenue growth and improve operating efficiencies, including effective resource utilization, information technology leverage and overhead cost management.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear focus on expansion and efficiency suggests strong long-term goals.

factors.summary: A specific catalyst is the expected completion of the sale of the Global Connect business in the second quarter of 2021.

evidence.text: The Transaction is subject to approval by our shareholders, regulatory approvals, consultation with the works council and other customary closing conditions; and it is expected to close in the second quarter of 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected completion of a sale could provide a significant catalyst.

factors.summary: The company's free cash flow generation appears to be mixed. While there are signs of improvement, such as increased cash flows from operations and better working capital management, there are also challenges due to the impact of COVID-19 and restructuring charges, which are putting pressure on cash flow.

evidence.text: Cash flows from operations were $662 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2020, an increase of $66 million primarily due to working capital timing and lower income tax payments and interest payments.

We have taken aggressive cost actions to date and continue to closely monitor the situation. We remain well-capitalized, have sufficient liquidity to satisfy our cash needs and will take additional actions as required.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from operational improvements and external pressures.

factors.summary: The company is addressing its high debt leverage through debt refinancing and lower interest rates, though there are risks associated with utilizing sale proceeds to reduce debt.

evidence.text: In June 2020, we entered into a Credit Agreement that provides for new dollar and euro term loan facilities. The proceeds were used to redeem higher interest rate notes and refinance existing debt. The new loans have lower interest rates compared to the previous ones.

the sale of Global Connect may not achieve the intended benefits. For example, we may be unable to utilize the proceeds from the Transaction as anticipated or capture the value we expect from our plans to reduce debt.
Our high level of indebtedness requires active management of our debt profile, with a focus on underlying maturities, interest rate risk, liquidity and operating cash flows.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Addressing debt through refinancing but risks remain.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, contributing to margin stability and improved financial performance. The initiatives are expected to generate $250 million in annual run-rate savings and drive permanent cost savings and operational efficiencies.

evidence.text: Restructuring charges totaled $145 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2020, reflecting ongoing cost management efforts.
We recorded $50 million in restructuring charges for the three months ended September 30, 2020. These charges primarily related to severance costs associated with employee severance packages.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations – Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: We recorded $145 million in restructuring charges for the nine months ended September 30, 2020. These charges primarily related to severance costs associated with employee separation packages.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: We expect the Restructuring Plan to be substantially completed in 2020 and for restructuring actions and other permanent cost-savings initiatives to drive approximately $250 million in pre-tax annual run-rate savings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, contributing to margin stability and improved financial performance.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance across different periods and segments. While some periods and segments indicate improvement or stability, others highlight pressure and challenges, particularly related to restructuring charges and cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: Non-GAAP business segment income decreased 2.9% year-over-year but was flat on a constant currency basis, particularly for the Connect segment which saw no change in income.

Operating income for the three months ended September 30, 2020 was $174 million as compared to operating loss of $740 million for the three months ended September 30, 2019. Adjusted EBITDA increased 5.3% to $501 million for the three months ended September 30, 2020 from $476 million for the three months ended September 30, 2019.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations – Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: Operating income for the nine months ended September 30, 2020 was $300 million as compared to operating loss of $317 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2019. Non-GAAP business segment income increased 18.1% on a constant currency basis for the Connect segment and 2.6% for the Media segment.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: We expect 2020 pre-tax restructuring charges of $160 to $170 million.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance across different periods and segments, indicating some improvement but also challenges.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is mixed. While the evidence suggests that capital expenditures are maintained at a similar level, indicating confidence in future growth opportunities, the introduction section does not explicitly address the capital expenditure outlook.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures are maintained at a similar level, signaling confidence in future growth opportunities.
Investments in property, plant, equipment, software, and other assets totaled $345 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2020, similar to the previous year.
The following discussion and analysis should also be read together with the accompanying Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements and related notes thereto.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are maintained at a similar level, indicating confidence in future growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The company is committed to returning value to shareholders through consistent dividend payments and an active share repurchase program. The evidence highlights the substantial amounts allocated to dividends and the ongoing share repurchase authorization, reflecting management's confidence in the stock.

evidence.text: Under the dividend policy, $64 million and $373 million were paid in cash dividends for the years ended September 30, 2020 and 2019, respectively.
A share repurchase program of up to $2 billion remains in place.
evidence.subsection: Income Taxes (Topic 740): Simplifying the Accounting for Income taxes

evidence.text: Our Board approved a share repurchase program for up to $2 billion of our outstanding common stock...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is committed to returning value to shareholders through consistent dividend payments and an active share repurchase program.

factors.summary: The company's forward guidance reflects a cautiously optimistic stance, with management expressing confidence in future performance despite challenges. They anticipate sufficient liquidity and are focused on cost-saving measures, aiming to achieve significant annual savings through restructuring efforts.

evidence.text: The company expects cash flow from operations, combined with existing cash and revolving credit facility availability, to provide sufficient liquidity for obligations and growth.

We expect the Restructuring Plan to be substantially completed in 2020 and for restructuring actions and other permanent cost-savings initiatives to drive approximately $250 million in pre-tax annual run-rate savings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's forward guidance reflects a cautiously optimistic stance with confidence in future performance despite challenges.

factors.summary: The company experienced mixed trends in input costs, with temporary cost savings due to COVID-19 pandemic actions and productivity initiatives, partially offset by investments in services and product portfolio management. Input costs overall remained stable, with no significant changes in raw materials, commodities, or components.

evidence.text: The decrease in cost of revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2020 as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2019 was primarily due to temporary costs savings due to actions taken in response to the COVID-19 pandemic and our productivity initiatives, partially offset by global investments in our services, including retailer investments.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations – Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: The following discussion and analysis should also be read together with the accompanying Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements and related notes thereto.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed trends in input costs with temporary savings offset by investments, overall stable.

factors.summary: The company faces significant foreign exchange exposure primarily to the Euro, which affects its financial results through both translation and transaction effects. Foreign exchange movements have resulted in net losses, indicating a negative impact on margins.

evidence.text: Our financial results are reported in U.S. dollars and are therefore subject to the impact of movements in exchange rates on the translation of the financial information of individual businesses whose functional currencies are other than U.S. dollars. Our principal foreign exchange revenue exposure is spread across several currencies, primarily the Euro.

The average U.S. Dollar to Euro exchange rate was $1.12 to €1.00 for each of the nine months ended September 30, 2020 and 2019. We realized net losses of $6 million and $10 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2020 and 2019, respectively, resulting primarily from fluctuations in certain foreign currencies associated with intercompany transactions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant foreign exchange exposure primarily to the Euro, resulting in net losses and negative impact on margins.

factors.summary: The economic environment presents significant headwinds due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. Uncertainties regarding the speed of recovery, particularly in sectors like retail measurement services and sports, are affecting both credit risk and reserve levels. The situation remains challenging, with potential impacts on future periods' financial forecasts.

evidence.text: The uncertainty regarding the length of lock-downs related to the COVID-19 pandemic and speed of recovery may impact our level of reserves in future periods. We continue to monitor and assess the impacts related to our different clients and will base our reasonable forecasts on the latest information available.
The overall economic environment remains challenging due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on retail measurement services, sports, and other sectors.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic headwinds due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and uncertainties affecting credit risk and reserve levels.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating across both the Connect and Media segments, with the impact of COVID-19 being a significant contributing factor. Despite slight improvements in constant currency terms for Media, the overall trend remains negative.

evidence.text: Revenues within our Connect segment decreased 2.5%, or a decrease of 1.6% on a constant currency basis. Revenues within our Media segment decreased 3.9%, or a decrease of 4.2% on a constant currency basis.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations – Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: Revenues within our Connect segment decreased 5.6%, or a decrease of 3.0% on a constant currency basis. Revenues within our Media segment decreased 2.5%, or a decrease of 2.2% on a constant currency basis.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: For Nielsen Global Media, the health of our consumer panels which are a very important source of measurement data have and will continue to be impacted because our field workers are constrained from their activities relating to recruiting, metering and monitoring the panelist homes. For Nielsen Global Connect, this was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on retail measurement services in markets that are heavy in traditional trade as well as pressures on custom insights and physical work conducted in stores innovation. These pressures are expected to continue, primarily due to non-contracted revenues in both Nielsen Global Media and Nielsen Global Connect.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating across both segments, with significant impact from COVID-19.

factors.summary: The company has experienced significant activity related to material acquisitions and divestitures, including the sale of its Global Connect business and minimal spending on smaller acquisitions that do not materially impact its consolidated results.

evidence.text: On October 31, 2020, we entered into an agreement to sell our Global Connect business to affiliates of Advent International Corporation...

For the nine months ended September 30, 2020, we paid cash consideration of $29 million associated with current period acquisitions, net of cash acquired. Had these 2020 acquisitions occurred as of January 1, 2020, the impact on our consolidated results of operations would not have been material.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company has significant M&A activity, but the evidence suggests mixed impact on results.

factors.summary: The company faces multiple key sensitivities, primarily centered on the successful completion of the Global Connect sale, regulatory approvals, and risks related to data privacy and security. Additionally, the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on operations and demand remains a significant factor affecting near-term results.

evidence.text: The Transaction is subject to conditions, including regulatory approvals... The success of the sale and the company's ability to reduce debt are significant factors.

Our growth objective of geographic market and service expansion requires us to maintain the consistency and integrity of our information and underlying processes on a global scale, and to invest effectively our capital in technology and infrastructure to keep pace with our clients’ demands and our competitors.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple significant risks including regulatory approvals and ongoing pandemic impacts.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing impacts from the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly affecting sports and local television markets, as well as the completion of the sale of the Global Connect business, which is subject to various approvals and conditions.

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues was driven primarily by the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports and non-contracted revenue and ongoing pressure in local television.

The Transaction is subject to approval by our shareholders, regulatory approvals, consultation with the works council and other customary closing conditions; and it is expected to close in the second quarter of 2021.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing COVID-19 impacts and dependency on Global Connect sale completion.

factors.summary: Demand trends are decelerating across the company, particularly influenced by the ongoing effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. This deceleration is evident in both the Revenues section and the Business Segment Overview, with specific challenges faced by Nielsen Global Media and Nielsen Global Connect. The pandemic has impacted field activities and retail measurement services, leading to reduced revenues and continued pressures on custom insights and physical store innovations.

evidence.text: Revenues from Measure decreased 1.9% to $520 million, or a decrease of 0.6% on a constant currency basis, reflecting some continued but lessening impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on retail measurement services. Revenues from Predict/Activate decreased 4.2% to $207 million, on a reported and constant currency basis, reflecting the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly in custom insights, partially offset by the January 2020 acquisition of Precima.

For Nielsen Global Media, the health of our consumer panels which are a very important source of measurement data have and will continue to be impacted because our field workers are constrained from their activities relating to recruiting, metering and monitoring the panelist homes. For Nielsen Global Connect, this was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on retail measurement services in markets that are heavy in traditional trade as well as pressures on custom insights and physical work conducted in stores innovation. These pressures are expected to continue, primarily due to non-contracted revenues in both Nielsen Global Media and Nielsen Global Connect.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to COVID-19 effects across multiple segments.

factors.summary: End markets are facing significant challenges, particularly in media, retail, sports, and local television, due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. Both Audience Measurement and Plan/Optimize revenues have declined, reflecting pressure on non-contracted revenues and constrained field activities.

evidence.text: Revenues from Audience Measurement decreased 0.9%, or a decrease of 0.7% on a constant currency basis, reflecting the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports and non-contracted revenue and ongoing pressure in local television.
Plan/Optimize revenues decreased 6.5%, or a decrease of 6.1% on a constant currency basis, primarily reflecting the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports, Gracenote auto and short-cycle revenue.

For Nielsen Global Media, the health of our consumer panels which are a very important source of measurement data have and will continue to be impacted because our field workers are constrained from their activities relating to recruiting, metering and monitoring the panelist homes.

For Nielsen Global Connect, this was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on retail measurement services in markets that are heavy in traditional trade as well as pressures on custom insights and physical work conducted in stores innovation.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: End markets face significant challenges from the pandemic, leading to revenue declines.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are positively impacting efficiency, primarily through cost reductions and restructuring actions, with expected annual savings of $250 million. However, the evidence suggests mixed results, as some cost decreases are due to temporary COVID-19 related actions rather than sustained productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Productivity initiatives are improving efficiency, particularly in reducing costs within segments.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: Costs within our Connect segment decreased 3.9%, or a decrease of 1.9% on a constant currency basis, primarily due to temporary actions taken in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, our productivity initiatives and lower variable costs associated with lower revenues.

Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased 0.9% to $1,417 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2020 from $1,430 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2019, or an increase of 0.5% on a constant currency basis.

We expect the Restructuring Plan to be substantially completed in 2020 and for restructuring actions and other permanent cost-savings initiatives to drive approximately $250 million in pre-tax annual run-rate savings.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed results with temporary COVID-19 impacts and some positive productivity gains.
